title: Arianespace opens door to GEO rideshare opportunities from 2029
summary:

title: NASA’s New Horizons Spacecraft Wakes from Hibernation in Good Health
summary: Following its longest hibernation period ever of nearly a year, NASA’s New Horizons spacecraft has emerged in good health and is ready to begin transmitting science data gathered in the distant Kuiper Belt far beyond Pluto. On June 23, flight controllers at the Johns Hopkins Applied Physics Laboratory (APL) in Laurel, Maryland, confirmed New Horizons, […]

title: Artemis II Crew and Apollo 14 Moon Tree
summary: In this photograph, the Artemis II crew participates in the dedication of the Apollo 14 Moon tree at the Lunar Receiving Park at NASA’s Johnson Space Center on June 25, 2026. This tree is a second-generation Apollo Moon tree of the loblolly pine species. The original Apollo Moon trees were grown from seeds carried aboard […]

title: SpaceX launches Transporter-17 amid concerns about rideshare program’s future
summary:
SpaceX launched the latest in its Transporter series of rideshare missions July 7 as industry concerns about the program’s future reach what one rival company executive called a panic.

title: Isar Aerospace Signs US$112.5M Deal for Dedicated Launch Site in Canada
summary:
German rocket builder Isar Aerospace has signed an agreement with Canada’s Maritime Launch Services to develop a dedicated launch facility at Spaceport Nova Scotia. The first launch of an Isar Aerospace Spectrum rocket from the site is expected in 2028. Announced on 7 July, the agreement will see Maritime Launch Services provide a licensed launch […]
The post Isar Aerospace Signs US$112.5M Deal for Dedicated Launch Site in Canada appeared first on European Spaceflight.

title: Simera Sense to provide imagers for Canary Islands constellation
summary:
Belgium’s Simera Sense has won a contract to provide multispectral imagers for a small constellation being designed to track wildfires, volcanic activity and other regional priorities for the Canary Islands off the coast of Spain.
The post Simera Sense to provide imagers for Canary Islands constellation appeared first on SpaceNews.

title: Isar Aerospace signs agreement for Canadian launch site
summary:
Isar Aerospace has signed a contract to develop a Canadian launch site for its Spectrum rocket, an agreement linked to a deal to sell submarines to the Canadian military.

title: Unpacking Europe’s new weather imagers
summary: The second Meteosat Third Generation Imager satellite (MTG-I2) has begun its launch campaign, during which it will go through final inspections before being fitted inside the fairing of the Ariane 6 rocket that will launch it into space towards the end of the summer. So how will this 3800 kg satellite help improve weather forecasts for Europe and North Africa?
